title_en: Red Dye 3: Why Did It Take 35 Years?
hook_en: Watch out! This candy could be toxic!
key_message: Red Dye 3, banned in cosmetics since 1990, is finally banned in food by 2025 due to its carcinogenic properties.
visual_elements: cut to close-up of her holding a candy with vibrant red coloring
split screen showing a timeline from 1990 to 2025
text overlay: '2025: Finally banned in food'
_source_regulation_context:
KEY REGULATORY EVENTS:
  • 2025: FDA finalizes ban on Red Dye 3 in food — Impact: Known carcinogen since 1990 — took 35 years to ban in food (was banned in cosmetics in 1990)

US OBESITY RATES OVER TIME:
  • 1960: 13.4% (adults), 5.0% (children)
  • 1980: 15.0% (adults), 5.5% (children)
  • 1994: 22.5% (adults), 10.0% (children)
  • 2000: 30.5% (adults), 13.9% (children)
  • 2010: 35.7% (adults), 16.9% (children)
  • 2016: 39.8% (adults), 18.5% (children)
  • 2020: 41.9% (adults), 19.7% (children)
  • 2024: 43.0% (adults), 21.0% (children)

hook_en: Did you know? Deregulation might be making us obese!
key_message: US deregulation has contributed to rising obesity rates by allowing more unhealthy additives in processed foods.
visual_elements: start with her pointing to a chart of obesity rates from 1960 to 2024
highlight key points where obesity spikes align with deregulation periods
pan over processed food packages with ingredient lists
